currentFAQs.q: How much does it cost to paint a house exterior in {{CITY}}?
currentFAQs.a: Most homeowners in {{CITY}} pay between {{AVG_LOW}} and {{AVG_HIGH}} for exterior painting, depending on home size and paint quality.

currentFAQs.q: How many coats of paint does an exterior need?
currentFAQs.a: Most exterior painting jobs require at least two coats for proper coverage and durability. A single coat may work for touch-ups or if the existing paint is in good condition with a similar color.

currentFAQs.q: How long does exterior paint last?
currentFAQs.a: Quality exterior paint typically lasts 5-10 years depending on climate, sun exposure, and paint quality. Premium paints like Sherwin-Williams Duration can last 10-15 years.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: How much does exterior painting cost in {{CITY}}?
Why is painting more | less expensive in {{CITY}}?
What paint type holds up best in {{CITY}}'s climate?
proposedFAQs.note: UV index + humidity + freeze-thaw drive paint chemistry choice. Climate-distinct.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: When is the best season to paint a {{CITY}} home?
proposedFAQs.note: Painting has the tightest temperature/humidity window of any trade — seasonNote is highest-impact here.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: What red flags should I watch for hiring a painter in {{CITY}}?
Does my {{CITY}} home need EPA RRP lead-paint precautions?
proposedFAQs.note: EPA RRP rule applies to pre-1978 homes. With avgHomeAge from city-context.json, we can give a city-distinct yes/no/probably.
